dims.relevance: Engages the right variables (lr, bs) and objective, topically on-target, but the framing actively misdirects.
dims.correctness: Wrong on every load-bearing claim — caps lr near 1.38e-3, predicts loss rises above it, places optimum at bs=256, whereas truth is lr=7.81e-3 / bs=1024 in the high-lr large-bs corner.
dims.anticipates_interaction: Fails the pivotal test — treats lr as a fixed U-shaped optimum independent of bs and explicitly says to fix lr near 1.38e-3 and sweep bs, the textbook freeze, rather than recognizing they must rise jointly toward the corner.
dims.actionability: Highly actionable but in the wrong direction — concrete first pairs and an explicit 'isolate batch-size by fixing lr' protocol that hard-codes the trap.

orienter.error_note: The error is seeded here: the prior invents a hard lr ceiling at ~1.38e-3 and prescribes fixing lr to sweep bs, directing the actor straight into the confident-wrong minimum and away from the high-lr/large-bs corner.
orienter.decision_error_or_information_gap: Decision error — with zero experiments the interaction (large batches tolerate higher lr) was knowable from priors, yet the Orienter asserted a confident lr ceiling and an explicit freeze protocol instead of hedging toward joint escalation.
orienter.bifurcation: Whether the orientation set up joint lr-bs escalation toward the corner or seeded an axis-freeze — and it decisively did the latter, both capping lr and prescribing the fix-lr/sweep-bs protocol.
